dtcs.description: Battery Voltage High SRSCM (Supplemental Restraints System Control Module) checks input voltage when "IG ON" to make air bag system work properly. If input voltage is out of normal range, there can be malfunction in system operation. In this case, Check battery and charging system. The SRSCM sets DTC B1101 and turns warning light on if voltage above threshold value is detected for more than 4 sec. (If voltage within threshold value is detected When the SRSCM returns to normal condition. SRSCM regards DTC as being cleared and turns warning light off). DTC Detecting Condition Specification Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Airbag(Event #1)

steps.instruction: Ignition "OFF", connect scantool.
Ignition "ON"& Engine "OFF", select "Current Data" mode.
Monitor the "Battery Voltage" parameter on the Scantool. Specification :Approx. 9 <= Vbatt <= 16 Fig.1) Normal Data
Is parameter displayed within specifications? W/Harness Inspection Airbag(Event #1)
Measure voltage between power terminal of the SRSCM harness connector and chassis ground. Specification :Approx. 9 <= Vbatt <= 16
Is the measured voltage within specifications? Ground Circuit Inspection

dtcs.description: Air Mix Potentiometer Open (Low)-Passenger The Air mix actuator(Temperature actuator) located at heater unit. It contains temp motor that changes temp door position and potentiometer that monitors position of temp door. The Air mix actuator(Temperature actuator) regulates the temperature by the procedure as follows. Signal from control unit adjusts position of temp door by operating temp motor and then temperature will be regulated by the hot/cold air ratio decided by position of temp door. In operation, potentiometer delivers temp door position transformed into voltage value to A/C ECU.

steps.instruction: Connect scantool with diagnostic connector.
Warm up the engine to normal temperature after engine start.
Select "Air Mix Door Potentiometer-Passenger" parameter on the current data with scantool.
Perform Actuation Test for "Passenger Air Mix Door - 0% / 50% / 100%.

With performing Actuation test, check that the value of Air Mix Door Potentiometer is changed and close to the value of Actuation Test. Specification :Check that the value of Air Mix Door Potentiometer at current data should be close to the value of the acutation test.
Does the value of current data follow in accordance with the each actuation test ? W/Harness Inspection Air Conditioner

dtcs.description: Intake Potentiometer Open (Low) The actuator contains a motor that changes intake door position and a potentiometer that monitors position of the door. When the driver changes the air intake switch, the ECU operates intake door motor to turn the intake door to the intended position. (in the FRESH mode, the intake door is closed. In REC mode, the intake door is opened) During operation the potentiometer delivers an intake door position to the A/C ECU.

steps.instruction: Connect scantool with diagnostic connector.
Warm up the engine to normal temperature after engine start
Select " Intake Potentiometer " parameter on the current data with scantool
Perform Actuation Test for Air Inlet Mode Selection - Reculation /Fresh in order.
With performing Actuation test, check that the value of each position sensors are changing. Specification :Recirculation : About 90%, Fresh : About 10%.
Are the value of each position sensors changed when performing actuation test ? W/Harness Inspection Air Conditioner

dtcs.description: Humidity Sensor Short (Low) - AUTO Defog The Auto Defogger sensor is installed on front window glass. The Auto Defogger sensor senses any moisture on the interior of the windshield.While controlling the temperature and Mode(direction of air) set by driver, if the humidity is higher than certain level, the Air condtioner control Module automatically controls auto defogging mode. The Air conditioner Control Module sets DTC B1281 if The signal from auto defogger sensor has been detected short to ground in ground circuit for 2 seconds. dtcs.description: Require Weight Check In contrast to the initial one-stage airbag systems, newer restraint systems involve complex logic to select, or alternatively suppress, various levels of safety system deployment. Inherent to an advanced Restraint System is the ability to discern information regarding passenger occupancy. It is intended that these inputs be provided through the weight measuring OC system. The object of such safety system is to reduce the risk and level of injuries by automatically adapting the airbag(s) and seat belt pretensioner to the driving status of the vehicle, its occupants, and the crash severity. The current OC system covered in this specification continually senses and classifies the front passenger side seat. OCS is utilizing a robust weight measuring technology. Thus, if any of the following conditions occur, 'command zero' and 'accuracy check' should be performed after checking WCS Diagnostic Codes. The weight classification ECU or any of the sensors is replaced. The vehicle is brought to the repair shop due to an accident or a crash even though the severity seems to be minor. The passenger airbag ON indicator is not illuminated when the passenger seat is not occupied. The passenger airbag ON indicator is delayed more than 10 seconds to be turned off when an adult passenger seats in. The passenger seat is removed from the vehicle and re-assembled. Any accessories (side table, seatback table and seat cover, etc) are replaced or installed. This DTC demand to check the accuracy of WCS at crash about air bag deployment or unusual Wight Status. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Weight Classification

Ignition "ON"& Engine "OFF", select "Diagnostic Trouble Codes(DTC)" mode.
Monitor diagnostic trouble code and present of trouble code.

Using a scan tool, Doing WCS Reset
Monitor diagnostic trouble code and present of trouble code.

dtcs.description: ODS ECU Defect In contrast to the initial one-stage airbag systems, newer restraint systems involve complex logic to select, or alternatively suppress, various levels of safety system deployment. Inherent to an advanced Restraint System is the ability to discern information regarding passenger occupancy. It is intended that these inputs be provided through the weight measuring OC system. The object of such safety system is to reduce the risk and level of injuries by automatically adapting the airbag(s) and seat belt pretensioner to the driving status of the vehicle, its occupants, and the crash severity. The current OC system covered in this specification continually senses and classifies the front passenger side seat. OCS is utilizing a robust weight measuring technology. Thus, if any of the following conditions occur, 'WCS Reset' and 'accuracy check' should be performed after checking WCS Diagnostic Codes. The weight classification ECU or any of the sensors is replaced. The vehicle is brought to the repair shop due to an accident or a crash even though the severity seems to be minor. The passenger airbag ON indicator is not illuminated when the passenger seat is not occupied. The passenger airbag ON indicator is delayed more than 10 seconds to be turned off when an adult passenger seats in. The passenger seat is removed from the vehicle and re-assembled. Any accessories (side table, seatback table and seat cover, etc) are replaced or installed. The WCS sets DTC B1344 if there is any fault in Weight Classification System Faulty WCS ECU. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Weight Classification

dtcs.description: Driver Airbag Resistance too High (1st stage) Driver Air bag module (hereinafter referred to DAB) located at center of steering wheel protects driver by reducing impact of collision. DAB is consist of air bag, pat cover and two inflator. There are power,circuit for ignition, gas generator and diffuser screen in inflator. Air bag reduces impact of collision by fillied up gas. In collision, pat cover splits and through this crack, air bag emerges and deploys. Inflator generates gas that expands air bag. Clock spring is located between steering wheel and column. It connects SRSCM to DAB. The SRSCM sets DTC B1346 if the measured resistance value of DAB circuit is more than the threshold value. *In this case, SRSCM checks if there's any fault in circuit by sending current for a while. DTC Detecting Condition Specification Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Airbag(Event #1)

steps.instruction: Ignition "OFF", connect scantool.
Ignition "ON"& Engine "OFF", select "Current Data" mode.

Monitor the "Driver airbag resistance" parameter on the Scantool. Specification :1.1Ohms< Resistance of driver airbag(1st stage) < 6.7Ohms Reference :In a case of an open in the driver airbag(1st stage) circuit : FAIL In a case of a short to battery in the driver airbag(1st stage) circuit: FAIL In a case of a short to ground in the driver airbag(1st stage) circuit : FAIL Fig.1) Normal Data
Is parameter displayed within specifications? W/Harness Inspection Airbag(Event #1)
Connect scantool to Data Link Connector(DLC) and clear DTC with scantool and diagnose again.
Is DTC present problem ? Clock Spring Circuit Inspection

dtcs.description: CAN Bus Off In contrast to the initial one-stage airbag systems, newer restraint systems involve complex logic to select, or alternatively suppress, various levels of safety system deployment. Inherent to an advanced Restraint System is the ability to discern information regarding passenger occupancy. It is intended that these inputs be provided through the weight measuring OC system. The object of such safety system is to reduce the risk and level of injuries by automatically adapting the airbag(s) and seat belt pretensioner to the driving status of the vehicle, its occupants, and the crash severity. The current OC system covered in this specification continually senses and classifies the front passenger side seat. OCS is utilizing a robust weight measuring technology. Thus, if any of the following conditions occur, 'command zero' and 'accuracy check' should be performed after checking WCS Diagnostic Codes. The weight classification ECU or any of the sensors is replaced. The vehicle is brought to the repair shop due to an accident or a crash even though the severity seems to be minor. The passenger airbag ON indicator is not illuminated when the passenger seat is not occupied. The passenger airbag ON indicator is delayed more than 10 seconds to be turned off when an adult passenger seats in. The passenger seat is removed from the vehicle and re-assembled. Any accessories (side table, seatback table and seat cover, etc) are replaced or installed. This code reports BUS OFF status when data transmit error count number is over 255. The purpose is to verify the status of CAN controller and CAN communication line when error is detected. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Weight Classification

steps.instruction: Ignition "OFF", connect scantool.
Ignition "ON"& Engine "OFF", select "Diagnostic Trouble Codes(DTC)" mode.
Monitor diagnostic trouble code and present of trouble code.
Using a scan tool, clear the DTC.
Is DTC present problem ? W/Harness Inspection Weight Classification
The 'WCS Reset' operation will take 3 - 10 second.
Make sure that there should not be vibration or weight adding to the passenger front seat.

If the 'WCS Reset' operation is finished successfully, you will see the following screen. dtcs.description: CAN time-out ACU In contrast to the initial one-stage airbag systems, newer restraint systems involve complex logic to select, or alternatively suppress, various levels of safety system deployment. Inherent to an advanced Restraint System is the ability to discern information regarding passenger occupancy. It is intended that these inputs be provided through the weight measuring OC system. The object of such safety system is to reduce the risk and level of injuries by automatically adapting the airbag(s) and seat belt pretensioner to the driving status of the vehicle, its occupants, and the crash severity. The current OC system covered in this specification continually senses and classifies the front passenger side seat. OCS is utilizing a robust weight measuring technology. Thus, if any of the following conditions occur, 'command zero' and 'accuracy check' should be performed after checking WCS Diagnostic Codes. The weight classification ECU or any of the sensors is replaced. The vehicle is brought to the repair shop due to an accident or a crash even though the severity seems to be minor. The passenger airbag ON indicator is not illuminated when the passenger seat is not occupied. The passenger airbag ON indicator is delayed more than 10 seconds to be turned off when an adult passenger seats in. The passenger seat is removed from the vehicle and re-assembled. Any accessories (side table, seatback table and seat cover, etc) are replaced or installed. This code is outputted when WCS can't receive data from SRSCM by CAN Line for 10 sec. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Weight Classification

Is DTC present problem ? W/Harness Inspection Weight Classification
The 'WCS Reset' operation will take 3 - 10 second.
Make sure that there should not be vibration or weight adding to the passenger front seat.

If the 'WCS Reset' operation is finished successfully, you will see the following screen. dtcs.description: P-SIS Front-Driver Defect Pressure-Side Impact Sensor and Side Impact Sensor are installed in the left and the right side of front door and in the lower of B-pillar respectively. The Pressure-Side Impact Sensor senses an impact based on an acceleration at crashing. Unlike the conventional acceleration sensor, the Pressure Sensor senses an air pressure by a distorted door at crashing and measures an impact. The SCM detects the impact signals of the Pressure-Side Impact Sensor and Side Impact Sensor and compares with signals of the safe sensor inside. If the signals of all sensors are judged as a collision, the side-airbag and the curtain-airbag would be unfolded. The SRSCM sets DTC B1739 if there is any fault in Pressure-Side Impact(Driver) circuit. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Airbag(Event #1)

dtcs.description: P-SIS Front-Driver Communication Error Pressure-Side Impact Sensor and Side Impact Sensor are installed in the left and the right side of front door and in the lower of B-pillar respectively. The Pressure-Side Impact Sensor senses an impact based on an acceleration at crashing. Unlike the conventional acceleration sensor, the Pressure Sensor senses an air pressure by a distorted door at crashing and measures an impact. The SCM detects the impact signals of the Pressure-Side Impact Sensor and Rear Side Impact Sensor and compares with signals of the safe sensor inside. If the signals of all sensors are judged as a collision, the side-airbag and the curtain-airbag would be unfolded. The SRSCM sets DTC B1742 if there is any error in communication between Pressure-Side Impact(Driver) and SRSCM. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Airbag(Event #1)

dtcs.description: Sub Micom Failed This is DTC which is related with communication error between PDM and other units. ** Body CAN communication Control Units : BCM(Body Control Module), CLU(Cluster), PDM(Power Distribution Module), SMK(Smart Key), SJB(Smart Junction Box). * CAN (Controller Area Network) : CAN is serial bus communication type which links not only communication system but also control units each other. This code reports the malfunction of NEC MCU inside PDM. This is the case which there is the NEC MCU communication error. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Power Distribution Module

steps.instruction: Connect GDS.
IG "ON" and engine "OFF".
Select "DTC Analysis" mode.
After erase DTC, keep the vehicle condition within "the enable conditions".
Is the same DTC occurred again ? W/Harness Inspection Power Distribution Module

dtcs.description: Immobilizer TX Short Circuit To Ground This is DTC which is related with communication error between PDM and other units. ** Body CAN communication Control Units : BCM(Body Control Module), CLU(Cluster), PDM(Power Distribution Module), SMK(Smart Key), RPAS(Rear Parking Assist System). * CAN (Controller Area Network) : CAN is serial bus communication type which links not only communication system but also control units each other. After insert the fob into the fob holder, press SSB button. Then, the communication state between the fob and the immobilizer is inputted to PDM. This code is outputted when the immobilizer data circuit is shorted to ground. (In this case, it is not possible to get authorization although the fob is in the holder.) DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Power Distribution Module

steps.instruction: Connect GDS.

After erase DTC, keep the vehicle condition within "the enable conditions" .
Is the same DTC occurred again ? Service Data Analysis

dtcs.description: Air Mix Motor-Driver The Air Mix actuator contains a motor that changes temp door position and a potentiometer that monitors position of temp door. The Air mix actuator(Temperature actuator) regulates the temperature by operating temp door motor. The potentiometer delivers temp door position to the A/C ECU. The Air conditioner Control Module sets DTC B2406 if Driver air mix actuator has not been moved to the mode,where air condition control module controls, within 40 seconds. DTC Detecting Condition Specification ** Voltage value of Air Mix potentiometer as a function of temp door position. Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Air Conditioner

steps.instruction: Connect scantool with diagnostic connector.
Warm up the engine to normal engine temperature after engine starts.
Select and monitor "Air Mix Door Potentioner-Driver" parameter on scantool.
Select and perform Actuation test Air Mix Door Potentioner-Driver - 0% / 50% / 100% in order.

Check that the value of all the parameters are changed when performing the actuation test. Specification :Check that the value of Air Mix Door Potentiometer at current data should be close to the value of the acutation test.
Does the value of current data follow in accordance with the each actuation test ? W/Harness Inspection Air Conditioner

dtcs.description: Intake Motor The actuator contains a motor that changes intake door position and a potentiometer that monitors position of the door. When the driver changes the air intake switch, the ECU operates intake door motor to turn the intake door to the intended position. (in the FRESH mode, the intake door is closed. In REC mode, the intake door is opened) During operation the potentiometer delivers an intake door position to the A/C ECU. The Air conditioner Control Module sets DTC B2408 if Intake actuator has not been moved to the mode,where air condition control module controls, within 40 seconds. DTC Detecting Condition Specification ** Voltage value of Intake potentiometer as a function of position of Intake door Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Air Conditioner

steps.instruction: Connect scantool with diagnostic connector.
Warm up the engine to normal temperature after engine start
Select " Intake Potentiometer " parameter on the current data with scantool.
Perform Actuation Test for Air Inlet Mode Selection - Reculation /Fresh in order.
With performing Actuation test, check that the value of each position sensors are changing. Specification :Recirculation : About 90%, Fresh : About 10%.
Are the value of each position sensors changed when performing actuation test ? W/Harness Inspection Air Conditioner

dtcs.description: Direction Control Motor-Driver The mode control actuator mounted on heater unit adjusts position of mode door by operating Direction Motor in accordance with signal of A/C control unit. Pressing mode select switch makes the mode control actuator shift in order of vent-> B/L -> floor -> mix. The Air conditioner Control Module sets DTC B2409 if Driver Direction actuator has not been moved to the mode,where air condition control module controls, within 40 seconds. DTC Detecting Condition Specification ** Voltage value of potentiometer as a function of mode door position. Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Air Conditioner

steps.instruction: Connect scantool with diagnostic connector.
Warm up the engine to normal engine temperature after engine starts.
Select and monitor "Direction Potention" parameter on scantool.
Select and perform Actuation test Driver Mode Door - Face / Foot / Defrost in order.
Check that the value of all the parameters are changed when performing the actuation test. Specification :Face - About below 10%, Foot : About 50%, Defrost : About 90%.
Are all the parameters changed when performing Actuation test ? W/Harness Inspection Air Conditioner

dtcs.description: Air Mix Door Motor-Passenger The Air mix actuator(Temperature actuator) located at heater unit. It contains temp motor that changes temp door position and potentiometer that monitors position of temp door. The Air mix actuator(Temperature actuator) regulates the temperature by the procedure as follows. Signal from control unit adjusts position of temp door by operating temp motor and then temperature will be regulated by the hot/cold air ratio decided by position of temp door. In operation, potentiometer delivers temp door position transformed into voltage value to A/C ECU. The Air conditioner Control Module sets DTC B2415 if passenger air mix actuator has not been moved to the mode,where air condition control module controls, within 40 seconds. DTC Detecting Condition Specification ** Voltage value of Air Mix potentiometer as a function of temp door position. Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Air Conditioner

steps.instruction: Connect scantool with diagnostic connector.
Warm up the engine to normal temperature after engine start.
Select "Air Mix Door Potentiometer-Passenger" parameter on the current data with scantool.
Perform Actuation Test for "Passenger Air Mix Door - 0% / 50% / 100% in order.

With performing Actuation test, check that the value of each position sensors are changing. Specification :Check that the value of Air Mix Door Potentiometer at current data should be close to the value of the acutation test.
Does the value of current data follow in accordance with the each actuation test ? W/Harness Inspection Air Conditioner

dtcs.description: MM CAN Bus Off Error The CAN Communication consists of a twisted pair of network wires. One CAN low line and one CAN high line. The CAN lines pass through the HeadUnit and AMP modules. This allows the TMU to communicate with each module. TMU exchange messages with headunit, MPG from cluster for eco coach service and GPS data from AVN headunit for LBS service throught the CAN. The TMU sets B2800 when CAN line is in the below status. - Short to Ground - Short to Battery DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Telematics Unit

Read the "DTC Status" parameter.

dtcs.description: GPS Module Connection Error GPS (Global Positioning System) is the only fully functional Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS). Utilizing a constellation of at least 24 Medium Earth Orbit satellites that transmit precise microwave signals, the system enables a GPS receiver to determine its location, speed, direction, and time. GPS is only for TMU Audio Module. (not in AVN TMU but in AVN headunit) To provide LBS(Location based service) or Emergency service, TMU (Audio only) has GPS module in the circuit. The TMU sets B2837 when GPS antenna has been disconeccted or the communication is interrupted between main CPU and GPS module. With this failure, no LBS or TBT (Turn by turn navigation) would be functional. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Telematics Unit

dtcs.description: CDMA Modem Fail TMU can get a phone call from Call Center. To communicate with Call center, TMU should support CDMA (Code Division Multiple Access) functionality. CDMA is a channel access method utilized by various radio communication technologies. If CDMA modem is fail, no connection with call center would be made. The B2859 is SET when internal communication between main CPU with Modem chip. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Telematics Unit
